message:
fix(layout): correct force-directed physics and Tarjan SCC, add proptest coverage across all crates

This commit addresses several correctness bugs in the layout engine and
adds comprehensive property-based testing across all five core crates.

**Layout engine fixes (fm-layout):**
- Fix Fruchterman-Reingold attractive force formula: the force between
  connected nodes was computed as `dist / k` instead of the correct
  `(dist * dist) / k`. This under-attracted connected nodes, producing
  layouts where edges were longer than necessary and clusters were too
  spread out. All 8 golden SVG files are updated to reflect the corrected
  node positions.
- Fix Tarjan SCC lowlink propagation: after recursing into a successor,
  the lowlink was incorrectly pulled from `indices[next]` (which could
  be None in edge cases) instead of the already-computed `lowlink[next]`.
  This could cause strongly connected components to be split incorrectly,
  leading to missed cycle detection and suboptimal rank assignment.
- Fix rank_assignment singleton filtering: components of length 1 were
  being unconditionally skipped (`continue` when `len <= 1`), which
  silently dropped single-node connected components that had edges.
  Now only truly isolated singletons (zero incident edges) are deferred.
- Remove Copy derive from CycleClusterMap since it holds Vec fields —
  this was only compiling because the struct was never copied, but it
  would cause silent deep-copy performance issues if it ever were.
- Rename layout_diagram_radial_traced to _dup to resolve a duplicate
  symbol conflict with the existing radial layout path.
- Remove unnecessary explicit lifetime annotation on layout_label_text.
- Apply rustfmt to several multi-argument function calls that were
  previously formatted as single long lines.

**Property-based testing (all crates):**
- fm-parser: proptest that parse() is total (never panics on arbitrary
  input up to 256 chars) with bounded confidence [0,1], and that
  detect_type_with_confidence is deterministic across repeated calls.
- fm-layout: proptest that chain graphs of 1..20 nodes produce
  deterministic layouts with non-overlapping node bounds, and that
  layout stats (edge lengths, bounds) are non-negative.
- fm-render-svg: proptest that render_svg is total for 0..20 node linear
  graphs, produces valid SVG wrapping, and data-nodes/data-edges
  attributes match the input graph size.
- fm-render-term: proptest that terminal rendering respects the requested
  column/row bounds for arbitrary graph sizes.
- fm-core: exhaustive unit tests for all enum string mappings (diagram
  types, directions, arrow types, node shapes, palettes, error codes,
  warning codes, severity levels, diagnostic categories), config
  roundtrips, IR helpers, span builders, and structured diagnostic
  construction — ensuring these public API surfaces remain stable.

**Golden test updates:**
- All 8 golden SVG files regenerated to match the corrected force-
  directed layout positions. Changes are position/coordinate updates
  only; no structural SVG changes.

**Dependency additions:**
- proptest 1.6.0 added as dev-dependency to fm-parser, fm-layout,
  fm-render-svg, and fm-render-term.
- Cargo.lock updated with proptest and its transitive dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
